summary:
Connective tissue grafts may fail in treating large or deep recessions classified under Classes III and IV due to limited available donor site tissues required for successful repair. The success rate decreases significantly as the complexity and size of the defect increase beyond moderate levels.
The summary captures the critical aspects regarding the limitations faced during graft placements based on different types of gum recession classifications without exceeding two sentences. It highlights how effectiveness declines particularly within broader categories indicating higher degrees of dental issues needing intervention through such procedures. 

End of Summary
To ensure clarity:

1) I've maintained a focus strictly on clinically pertinent information related directly addressing where these specific surgical interventions might not succeed optimally given varying severities indicated across classification systems used medically today concerning periodontal conditions like those described here about receding gums treated via CTG surgery methods.

2) By limiting details only necessary for understanding why failures occur predominantly at greater depths/areas affected rather than detailing every single study finding mentioned earlier; thus condensing essential insights into just over one paragraph total lengthwise ensuring brevity yet comprehensive enough contextually speaking too!
